text: LLANFIHANGEL-ABERGWISSIN, a parish in Breconshire, 12 m. W by N of Builth. Pop. in 1831, 345; in 1841, 311.

LLANFIHANGEL-AR-ARTH, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 14 m. N by E of Carmarthen, on the S bank of the Teifi, including the hamlets of Cwm- Arlloes, Frô, Gwydd Crûg, Gwyddll, and Pencader. Pop. in 1831, 2,023; in 1841, 1,993.
LLANFIHANGEL-BACHALLETH, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 5 m. W by S of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1831, 332; in 1841, 333.
LLANFIHANGEL-BRYN-PABWAN, a parish in Breconshire, 53 m. NW of Builth, including the conshire, 11 m. NE of Abergavenny, on a branch of the Usk. Pop. in 1831, 1,103; in 1841, 1,039.
LLANFIHANGEL-FACH, or LLANFIHANGELHELYGEN, a parish in Radnorshire, 5½ m. SE by E of Rhayader. Pop. in 1831, 101; in 1841, 102.
LLANFIHANGEL-FECHAN, a chapelry in Breconshire, 5 m. N of Brecon. Pop. in 1831, 188; in 1841, 200.

LLANFIHANGEL-GENEUR-GLYNN, a parish in Cardiganshire, 4 m. NE by N of Aberystwith, including the townships of Caelan-y-Maesmor, Cyfoethy-Brenin, Cynill-Mawr, Henllys, Scybor-y-Coed, and Tyr-y-Mynach. Pop. in 1831, 3,576; in 1841, 3,838.
LLANFIHANGEL-GLYN-Y-MYFYR, a parish partly in Denbighshire, and partly in Merionethshire, 10 m. SW of Ruthin, on the river Alwin. It includes Cefynpost. Pop. in 1831, 452; in 1841, 427.
LLANFIHANGEL-IN-RUG, or LLANRUG, a parish in Carnaryonshire, 3 m. E of Carnaryon, on the river Sciont. Pop. in 1831, 1,204; in 1841, 1,760.

LLANFIHANGEL-LLETHYR-FROED, a parish in Cardiganshire, 7 m. N by W of Tregaron, including the townships of Lledrod-Isaf and Lledrod- Uchaf. Pop. in 1831, 1,213; in 1841, 1,149. LLANFIHANGEL-NANT-BRANE (UPPER and LOWER), a parish in Breconshire, 8 m. NW by W of Brecon, on a branch of the Usk. Pop. in 1831, 528; in 1841, 495.
LLANFIHANGEL-NANT-MELLAN, a parish in Radnorshire, 3 m. SW of New Radnor. Pop. in 1831, 284; in 1841, 419.
LLANFIHANGEL-PENBEDW, a parish in Cardiganshire, 7 m. W by S of Newcastle-in-Emlyn. Pop. in 1831, 353; in 1841, 343.
LLANFIHANGEL - RHOSICORN, a parish in LLA Carmarthenshire, 11 m. NW by N of Llandeilo- Fawr. Pop. in 1831, 683; in 1841, 709.
LLANFIHANGEL-RHYDITHON, a parish in Radnorshire, 111 m. E by S of Rhayader. Pop. in 1831, 350; in 1841, 337.
LLANFIHANGEL-TAL-Y-LLYNN, a parish in Breconshire, 5 m. E of Brecon, intersected by the Hay and Kington railway. Pop. in 1841, 151.
LLANFIHANGEL-TRE'R-BEIRDD, a parish in Anglesey, 2 m. SE by E of Llanerchymedd. Pop. in 1831, 360; in 1841, 373.
LLANFIHANGEL-TYNSYLWY, a parish in Anglesey, 4 m. N of Beaumaris. Pop. in 1841, 63.

LLANFIHANGEL-Y-BONT-FAEN, a parish in Glamorganshire, 24 m. SW of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1831, 53; in 1841, 50. LLANFIHANGEL-Y-CROYDDIN (ISAF and UCHAF), a parish in Cardiganshire, 7 m. SE by E of Aberystwith. Pop. in 1831, 1,971; in 1841, 2,102.
LLANFIHANGEL-YN-HOWYN, a chapelry in the parish of Rhôs-Colyn, Anglesey, 6 m. SE of Holyhead. Pop. in 1831, 153; in 1841, 200.

LLANFIHANGEL-Y-PENNANT, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 4 m. NW of Tremadoc. Pop. in 1831, 563; in 1841, 680.—Also a parish in Merionethshire, 8 m. SW by S of Dolgelly, on the river Desynwy. Pop. in 1831, 394; in 1841, 375.

LLANFIHANGEL - YSCEIFIOG, a parish in Anglesey, 6½ m. W by N of Bangor, on the river Cefni. Pop. in 1831, 663; in 1841, 947. LLANFIHANGEL-YSTRAD (Lower and Up- PER), a parish in Cardiganshire, 6 m. NW of Lampeter, on the W bank of the Aeron. Pop. in 1831,

LLANFOIST, a parish in Monmouthshire, 12 m. SW by W of Abergavenny, bounded on the N and E by the Usk, and crossed by the Brecon canal, and the Abergavenny and Hereford railway. Area 2,490 acres. Pop. in 1831, 891; in 1841, 1,500.
LLANFROTHEN, a parish in Merionethshire, 4 m. E by N of Tremadoc. Pop. in 1841, 853.
LLANFUGAIL, or LLANFIGAEL, a parish in Anglesey, 5 m. E of Holyhead. Pop. in 1841, 154.
LLANFWROG, a parish in Anglesey, 3½ m. E by N of Holyhead. Pop. in 1831, 266; in 1841, 267. -Also a p. in Denbighshire, adjacent to the town of Ruthin, on the river Clydd. Pop. in 1841, 1,554.

LLANFYLLIN, a parish and borough in Montgomeryshire, 15 m. NW by N of Montgomery, in the vale of the Cain, an affluent of the Vyrnwy. Pop. in 1831, 1,836; in 1841, 1,955. The limits of the borough are co-extensive with those of the p., which comprehends 12 townships. It is contributory to Montgomery, and one of the polling-places for the co.
LLANFYNNYD, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 7 m. NW of Llandeilo-Fawr. Pop. in 1841, 1,358.
LLANFYRNACH, a parish in the co. of Pembroke, 9 m. S by W of Newcastle-in-Emlyn. Pop. 1,049.
LLANGADFAN, a parish in Montgomeryshire, 61 m. NW of Llanfair, on the river Banwy. Pop. in 1831, 1,067; in 1841, 1,070.
LLANGADOCK, a parish and market-town in Carmarthenshire, 6 m. SW of Llandovery, on the river Teifi, including the hamlets of Above-Sawddo, Duffryn-Cydrich, and Gwinfe. Pop. in 1841, 2,604.
LLANGAFFO, a parish in Anglesey, 5 m. NW by N of Carnarvon. Pop. in 1831, 137; in 1841, 139.
LLANGAIN, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 33 m. S by W of Carmarthen, on the W bank of the Towy. Pop. in 1831, 423; in 1841, 403.
LLANGAMMARCH, a parish in Breconshire, 7 m. SW of Builth, on a branch of the Wye, including the hamlets of Pen-Bualt and Treflis. Pop. 1,062.
LLANGAN, a parish, partly in Carmarthenshire, and partly in Pembroke, 5 m. NE by E of Narberth. Pop. in 1831, 733; in 1841, 640.
LLANGANATE, a mountain of Ecuador, in the prov. of Chimborazo, 50 m. ENE of the mountain of Chimborazo, and about 40 m. SE of that of Cotopaxi. It is covered with perpetual snow.
LLANGANNA, a parish in Glamorganshire, 32 m. NW of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1841, 238.
LLANGANTEN, a parish in Breconshire, 23 m. W by N of Builth. Pop. in 1831, 179; in 1841, 177.
LLANGAR, a parish in Merionethshire, 13 m. SW of Corwen, on the river Dee. Pop. in 1841, 250.
LLANGARRAN, a parish in Herefordshire, 5 m. WSW of Ross, on a branch of the Wye. Area 5,890 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,125; in 1841, 1,175.
LLANGASTY-TALLYLLYN, a parish in Breconshire, 6 m. SE by E of Brecon, on the romantic lake of Llangorse. Pop. in 1831, 175; in 1841, 164.
LLANGATHEN, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 3 m. W of Llandeilo-Fawr, on the river Towy. Pop. in 1831, 1,182; in 1841, 1,108.
LLANGATTOCK, a parish in Monmouthshire, including the town of Caerleon. Area 2,750 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,392; in 1841, 1,440.
LLANGATTOCK-LLINGOED, a parish in Monmouthshire, 5\frac{1}{2} m. NE of Abergavenny. Area 1,730 acres. Pop. in 1831, 191; in 1841, 203.
LLANGATTOCK-NEAR-USK, a parish in Monmouthshire, 3 m. SSE of Abergavenny, on the river Usk. Area 1,260 acres. Pop. in 1841, 171.
LLANGATTOCK-VIBON-AVEL, a parish in Monmouthshire, 33 m. NW of Monmouth. Area 3,650 acres. Pop. in 1831, 449; in 1841, 503.
LLANGATTWG, or LLANGATTOCK, a parish in Breconshire, 13 m. SE by E of Brecon, and crossed by the Brecon canal. Pop. in 1841, 4,334.

LLANGEDWYN, or LLANGLYDWEN, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 9 m. NNE of Narberth. Pop. in 1831, 319; in 1841, 322.—Also a p. in Denbighshire, 7 m. SW by W of Oswestry, on the river Tanat. Pop. in 1831, 323; in 1841, 332.

LLANGEFELACH, or LLANGEVELACH, a parish in Glamorganshire, 4 m. NNW of Swansea, including the hamlets of Close, Mawr, Penderry, and Rhyndroy-Clydach. There are numerous copperworks and collieries in the p. Pop. in 1841, 9,394.

LLANGEFNI, a parish and market-town in Anglesey, 15 m. ESE of Holyhead, on the river Cefni. It unites with Beaumaris in returning a member to parliament, and is one of the polling-places for the eo. Pop. in 1831, 1,753; in 1841, 1,755.
LLANGEINOR-ON-THE-HILLS, or LLANGUINOR, a parish in Glamorganshire, 5 m. N by E of Bridgend. Pop. in 1831, 292; in 1841, 363.
LLANGEINWEN (ISAF and UCHAF), a parish in Anglesey, 2 m. W by N of Carnarvon. Pop. 943.
LLANGEITHO, a parish in Cardiganshire, 8 m. N by E of Lampeter, on the river Aeron. Pop. in 1841, 431.
LLANGELER, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 5 m. E by S of Newcastle-in-Emlyn, on the river Teifi. Pop. in 1831, 1,713; in 1841, 1,747.
LLANGELYNIN, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 25 m. SSW of Conwy. Pop. in 1831, 279; in 1841, 270.
LLANGELYNIN (HIGHER and LOWER), a parish in Merionethshire, 7 m. SSW of Barmouth, on the coast, at Cardigan bay. Pop. in 1841, 1,033.
LLANGENDEIRN, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 5 m. SE of Carmarthen. Pop. in 1841, 2,624. LLA
LLANGENNECH, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 3½ m. NE by E of Llanelly. Pop. in 1841, 893.
LLANGENNITH, a parish in Glamorganshire, 15 m. W of Swansea. Pop. in 1831, 367; in 1841, 436.
LLANGENNY, a parish in Breconshire, 2 m. SE of Crickhowel, on the river Usk. Pop. in 1841, 427.
LLANGERNYW, or LLANGERNIEW (LOWER and UPPER), a parish in Denbighshire, 7 m. NE of Llanrwst, on the river Elwy, including the chapelry of Branar and Marchaled. Pop. in 1841, 1,118.
LLANGEVIEW, a parish in Monmouthshire, 11/4 m. E of Usk. Pop. in 1831, 180; in 1841, 187.
LLANGIAN, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 64 m. SW of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1841, 1,144.
LLANGIBBY, a parish in Monmouthshire, 3 m. S by W of Usk. Area 3,700 acres. Pop. 535.
LLANGIRIGG, a parish in Montgomeryshire, 5 m. S of Llanidloes. Pop. in 1841, 195.
LLANGISTIOLUS, a parish in Anglesey, 2 m. S of Llangeffni. Pop. in 1831, 873; in 1841, 935.
LLANGOED, a parish in Anglesey, 3 m. NNE of Beaumaris. Pop. in 1831, 562; in 1841, 604.
LLANGOEDMAWR, a parish in Cardiganshire, 1 m. E by S of Cardigan, on the E bank of the Teifi. Pop. in 1831, 1,014; in 1841, 985.

LLANGOLLEN, a parish and market-town in Denbighshire, 13 m. SE of Ruthin, on the banks of the Dee, which is here crossed by a bridge of 5 arches. The great parliamentary Holyhead mailroad passes through the town, and the p. is intersected by the celebrated aqueduct of Pont-y-Sycylltan. See CHIRK. The town, situated in the romantic vale of L., is much resorted to by tourists, and contains several good houses. The manufacture of flannel is carried on here. Coal and lime are found in the vicinity. Valle Crucis abbey lies on the border of the p. L. is one of the polling-places for the co. Pop. in 1831, 4,498; in 1841, 4,906.
LLANGOLMAN, a parish in Pembrokeshire, 8 m. N of Narberth. Pop. in 1831, 411; in 1841, 255.
LLANGORSE (Lower and Upper), a parish in Breconshire, 6\frac{1}{2} m. SE by E of Brecon. Pop. 397.
LLANGOVEN, a parish in Monmouthshire, 53 m. SW by S of Monmouth. Pop. in 1841, 136.
LLANGOWER, a parish in Merionethshire, 3 m. S by E of Bala, on the E bank of Bala lake. Pop. in 1831, 412; in 1841, 368.
LLANCRANOG, a parish in Cardiganshire, 10 m. NE of Cardigan. Pop. in 1831, 921; in 1841, 884.
LLANGUA, a parish in Monmouthshire, 10 m. NE of Abergavenny, intersected by the Abergavenny and Hereford railway. Area 890 acres. Pop. 99.
LLANGWENLLWYFO, a parish in Anglesey, 45 m. SE by E of Amlwch. Pop. in 1841, 594.
LLANGWILLOG, a parish in Anglesey, 3 m. S by E of Llanerch-y-Medd. Pop. in 1841, 260.
LLANGWM, a parish in Denbighshire, 7 m. W by N of Corwen, on a branch of the Dee. Pop. 1,017.
LLANGWM (ICHA and UCHA), a parish in Monmouthshire, 3 m. E of Usk. Area 3,420 acres. Pop. in 1831, 292; in 1841, 350.—Also a p. in Pembrokeshire, 5 m. SE of Haverfordwest. Pop. 796.
LLANGWNODYL, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 11 m. W by S of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1841, 309.
LLANGWNOR, or LLANGUNNOR, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 1 m. E of Carmarthen, on the S bank of the Towy. Pop. in 1841, 1,229.
LLANGWYFAN, a parish in Anglesey, 10 m. SE of Holyhead. Pop. in 1831, 218; in 1841, 193. -Also a p. in Denbighshire, 5 m. E of Denbigh, on a branch of the Clydd. Pop. in 1841, 264.
LLANGWYSTENNIN, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 3 m. NE of Conwy. Pop. in 1841, 599.
LLANGYBI, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 5 m. NE by N of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1841, 726.
LLANGYBY, a parish in Cardiganshire, 4 m. NE by N of Lampeter. Pop. in 1841, 274.
LLANGYNELLO (Lower and Upper), a parish in Radnorshire, 5 m. W by S of Knighton. Pop. 444.
LLANGYNFELIN, a parish in Cardiganshire, 7 m. NE of Aberystwith. Pop. in 1841, 984.
LLANGYNHAFAL, a parish in Denbighshire, 3 m. N by E of Ruthin. Pop. in 1841, 502.
LLANGYNIN, or LLANDGINNING, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 10 m. W by S of Carmarthen, on the river Taff. Pop. in 1831, 434; in 1841, 405.
LLANGYNLLO, a parish in Cardiganshire, 4 m. NE of Newcastle-in-Emlyn. Pop. in 1841, 641.

LLANGYNNOG, or LLANGUNNOCH, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 6 m. SW of Carmarthen. Pop. in 1831, 859; in 1841, 800.—Also a p. in Montgomeryshire, 7 m. NW of Llanfillin. Pop. in 1831, 499; in 1841, 516.—Also a p. in Breconshire, 3\frac{1}{2} m. S by W of Builth. Pop. in 1831, 77; in 1841, 54.

LLANGYNWYD, or LLANGONOYD, a parish in Glamorganshire, 7 m. NNW of Bridgend, including the hamlets of Llangonoyd, Bayden, and Cwmdu. Pop. in 1831, 1,726; in 1841, 4,155, an increase attributable to the recent opening of iron and coalworks.
LLANGYNIDER, or LLANGUNNIDER, a parish in Breconshire, 10 m. NW by W of Abergavenny, on the S bank of the Usk, including the parcels of Blaine and Dyffryn, and Vro. Pop. in 1841, 2,775.
LLANGYNIEW, a parish in Montgomeryshire, 2½ m. NE of Llanfair, on the W bank of the Vyrnewy. Pop. in 1831, 675; in 1841, 647.
LLANHARAN, a parish in Glamorganshire, 3\frac{1}{2} m. NW of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1841, 306.
LLANHARY, a parish in Glamorganshire, 4 m. N of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1831, 208; in 1841, 238.
LLANHENNOCK, a parish in Monmouthshire, 13 m. NE by N of Caerleon. Area 1,450 acres. Pop. in 1831, 187; in 1841, 235.
LLANHILETH, or LLANHYDDELL, a parish in Monmouthshire, 10½ m. W by N of Usk. Area 2,030 acres. Pop. in 1831, 545; in 1841, 662.
LLANHIR, or LLANYEAR, a parish in Radnorshire, 7 m. SE of Rhayader, on the river Arun. Pop. in 1831, 675; in 1841, 746.
LLANHOWEL, a parish in Pembrokeshire, 45 m. NE by E of St. David's. Pop. in 1841, 160.
LLANHYCHAN, or LLANYCHAN, a parish in Denbighshire, 2½ m. N of Ruthin, on the river Clydd. Pop. in 1831, 115; in 1841, 111.
LLANIDAN, a parish in Anglesey, 5 m. NE of Carnarvon, including the chapelry of Llanfair-yn-y- Cwmmwd. Pop. in 1831, 969; in 1841, 1,409.

LLANIDLOES, a parish and borough in Montgomeryshire, 19 m. SW by W of Montgomery, on the Severn. The p., besides the borough, comprises the townships of Kilmarham, Manleth, Birthdir, Treflyn, and Cross-Lloybin, including large tracts of waste lands on the slope of Plinlimmon. Pop. in 1801, 2,282; in 1831, 4,189; in 1841, 4,261.—The town is situated in a pleasant vale watered by the Severn, and surrounded nearly on all sides by high hills. It consists of two principal streets crossing each other at right angles, and of several inferior ones. Many of the houses were formerly built with timber frames, the intervals being filled up by laths and mud, and some were roofed with slabs of oak timber cut into the form of slates. New erections, however, of better construction have superseded the older structures, and the town has assumed a more cheerful and agreeable aspect. The town-house is a massy building, erected in the old frame-work style. In the vicinity of the town are several handsome residences. Tourists generally commence their route to the steep Plinlimmon from this place. L. LLA was formerly a contributory borough to Montgomery. It is one of the polling-places for the co. The principal manufacture conducted in the town and vicinity is flannel, which has been extensively carried on from a very remote period. There are several factories for carding and spinning the wool, and fulling mills. In and around the town are several tanyards, flour-mills, and malt-houses.
LLANIESTYN, a parish in Anglesey, 2 m. NW of Beaumaris. Pop. in 1831, 313; in 1841, 275.— Also a parish in Carnarvonshire, 7 m. W by S of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1831, 1,115; in 1841, 1,096.
LLANIGON, a parish in Breconshire, 2 m. S of Hay, including the hamlet of Glyn-vach. Pop. 488.
LLANILAR (Lower and Upper), a parish in Cardiganshire, 5 m. SE of Aberystwith. Pop. 1,010.
LLANILLID, a parish in Glamorganshire, 4 m. NW of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1831, 119; in 1841, 148.
LLANILLTERN, a parochial chapelry in Glamorganshire, 6 m. NW of Cardiff. Pop. in 1841, 136.
LLANINA, a parish in Cardiganshire, 14 m. NW of Lampeter on the coast. Pop. in 1841, 447.
LLANIO, a township in the p. of Llandewi-Brefi, Cardiganshire, 7 m. SE of Lampeter. Pop. 150.
LLANISAN, a parish in Glamorganshire, 4 m. NNW of Cardiff. Pop. in 1831, 393; in 1841, 418.
LLANISHEN, a parish in Monmouthshire, 61 m. SSW of Monmouth. Area 1,570 acres. Pop. 307.
LLANLLAWDDOG, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 7½ m. N by E of Carmarthen. Pop. 779.
LLANLLAWER, a parish in Pembrokeshire, 2 m. SE by E of Fishguard. Pop. in 1841, 114.
LLANLLECHID, a parish in Carnaryonshire, 3 m. SE of Bangor. Pop. in 1831, 3,075; in 1841, 4,957.
LLANLLEONWELL, a parish in Breconshire, 7 m. W by S of Builth. Pop. in 1841, 261.
LLANLLOONY, or LLANLLWNY, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 9 m. SW of Lampeter, on the river Teifi. Pop. in 1831, 848; in 1841, 908.
LLANLLOWELL, a parish in Monmouthshire, 13 m. SSE of Usk, on the E bank of the Usk. Pop. in 1831, 78; in 1841, 109.
LLANLLUGGAN, or LLANLLIGAN, a parish in Montgomeryshire, 4 m. SW of Llanfair, on the river Rhiu. Pop. in 1831, 361; in 1841, 413.

LLANLLWCHAIARN, a parish in Cardiganshire, 2 m. S of Aberystwith. Pop. in 1831, 1,178; in 1841, 1,475.—Also a parish in Montgomeryshire, 11 m. NE of Newton, on the river Severn, including the townships of Aberbechan with Gwestyd, and Hendidley with Kilcowen. Pop. in 1841, 2,000.
LLANLLYFFNI, a parish in Carnaryonshire, 7 m. S of Carnarvon, on the river Llyfni. Pop. 2,017.
LLANMADOCK, a parish in Glamorganshire, 14 m. W by N of Swansea, at the mouth of the river Burry. Pop. in 1831, 240; in 1841, 269.
LLANMAES, a parish in Glamorganshire, 33 m. SSW of Cowbridge. Pop. in 1831, 234; in 1841, 194.
LLANMAREWIC, a parish in Montgomeryshire, 4 m. NE by E of Newton, on the SE bank of the Severn. Pop. in 1831, 201; in 1841, 167.
LLANMARTIN, a parish in Monmouthshire, 4 m. SE of Caerleon. Area 1,060 acres. Pop. 162.
LLANNEFYDD, or LLAN-UFYDD, a parish in Denbighshire, 53 m. NW of Denbigh, on the river Aled. Pop. in 1831, 1,130; in 1841, 1,195.
LLANNON, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 6 m. N by E of Llanelly, including the hamlets of Bleyne, Glyn, Goytre, and Ismorlais. Pop. in 1841, 1,769.
LLANNOR, or LLANFAWR, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 3 m. NW of Pwllheli. Pop. in 1841, 1,227.
LLANO-GRANDE, a town of New Grenada, in the dep. of Cauca, 78 m. NNE of Popayan, in a plain. LLANOS. See article AMERICA, vol. i. p. 215.
LLANOS (Los), a town on the SW coast of the island of Palma, Balearic islands. Pop. 4,989. Agriculture forms the chief branch of local industry.
LLANOVER, a parish in Monmouthshire, 31 m. S by E of Abergavenny, on the river Usk. Pop. 3,123.
LLANRHAIADAR-IN-KINMERCH, a parish in Denbighshire, 3 m. SE of Denbigh, on a branch of the Clydd. Pop. in 1831, 2,066; in 1841, 3,123.
LLANRHAIADAR-Y-MOCHNANT, a parish in Denbighshire, 4 m. N of Llanfyllin, on the river Tanat. Pop. in 1831, 2,344; in 1841, 2,620.
LLANRHIAN, a parish in Pembrokeshire, 5 m. NE by N of St. David's. Pop. in 1841, 912.
LLANRHIDIAN (HIGHER and LOWER), a parish in Glamorganshire, 10 m. W of Swansea. Pop. 1,760.
LLANRHIDIAN, or LLANRYTHAN, a parish in Pembrokeshire, 7½ m. NE by E of St. David's. Pop. in 1831, 141; in 1841, 182.
LLANRHYCHWYN, a parish in Carnarvonshire, 2 m. W by N of Llanrwst, on the river Conwy. Pop. in 1831, 565; in 1841, 551.
LLANRHYDD, a parish in Denbighshire, 13 m. W by S of Ruthin. Pop. in 1841, 840.
LLANRHYDDLAD, a parish in Anglesey, 5½ m. NE by N of Holyhead. Pop. in 1841, 725.
LLANRHYSTYD, a parish in Cardiganshire, 8 m. S by W of Aberystwith, on the coast of the Irish sea, including the townships of Haminiog and Mevenidd. Pop. in 1831, 1,525; in 1841, 1,608.
LLANRHYWYDRUS, a parish in Anglesey, 75 m. W of Amlwch, on the coast. Pop. in 1841, 158.
LLANROTHALL, a parish in Herefordshire, 5 m. N by W of Monmouth, on the river Monnow. Area 1,740 acres. Pop. in 1831, 128; in 1841, 108.

LLANRWST, a parish and market-town in Denbighshire, 17 m. WSW of Denbigh, on the banks of the Conwy, including the townships of Garthgarmon and Tybrith-Uchal. Pop., including that portion of the parish which extends into Carnarvonshire, in 1831, 3,601; in 1841, 3,905. The church has a beautiful lateral chapel, built after a design by Inigo Jones. Within the church is preserved the stone coffin in which the remains of Llewellyn, the last prince of Wales, were deposited. The town, which contains several good houses, forms a square in the centre of which stand the market-house and assembly-rooms. Lead ore of a very pure kind is raised in the vale of Llanrwst. It is one of the polling places for the co. members.
LLANSADWRN, a parish in Anglesey, 23 m. W of Beaumaris. Pop. in 1831, 371; in 1841, 452.
LLANSADWRN (Lower and Upper), a parish in Carmarthenshire, 2½ m. N by W of Llangaddoc, on a branch of the Teifi. Pop. in 1841, 1,192.
LLANSADWRNEN, a parish in Carmarthenshire, 1½ m. SW of Laugharne. Pop. in 1841, 237.

LLANSAINTFFRAED, a parish in Breconshire, 6 m. SE of Brecon, on the river Usk, and intersected by the Brecon canal. Pop. in 1831, 190; in 1841, 203.—Also a parish in Denbighshire, 13 m. SE by E of Conwy, on the E bank of the Conwy, near its mouth. Pop. in 1831, 1,306; in 1841, 1,286.—Also a parish in Montgomeryshire, 5 m. E by N of Llanfyllin, on the river Vyrnwy, including the townships of Colfryn, Llanarchkeela, Trewillan, and Trederwenfawr. Pop. in 1831, 1,365; in 1841, 1,399.— Also a parish in Monmouthshire, 43 m. SE of Abergavenny. Pop. in 1831, 24; in 1841, 20.
LLANSAINTFRAID - GLYNN - CEIRIOG, a parish in Denbighshire, 2 m. S by W of Llangollen, on the river Ceiriog. Pop. in 1831, 543; in 1841, 572.
LLANSAINTFFRAID-GLYN-DYDWY, a parish in Merionethshire, 2 m. E of Corwen, on the river Dee. Pop. in 1831, 60; in 1841, 180.
LLANSAINTFFRAID-IN-ELVEL, a parish in LLA Radnorshire, 5 m. NE of Builth. Pop. in 1831, 343; in 1841, 313.

LLANSAMLET (HIGHER and Lower), a parish in Glamorganshire, 4 m. NNE of Swansea, on the E bank of the Tawe. Pop. in 1801, 2,567; in 1831, 3,187; in 1841, 3,375, chiefly employed in coal and copper-works.
